SEARCH
Package server.control

Class ReservationController

java.lang.Object
server.control.ReservationController
All Implemented Interfaces:
DomainController
public class ReservationController extends Object implements DomainController
Owns the reservation & waiting-list domain (create/get/update/cancel/list, confirm, join/leave waitlist, accept grab). Stateless and shared across all client threads — only the final ReservationDAO collaborator is held as state.

Implemented: the read ops (RequestType.GET_RESERVATION, RequestType.LIST_RESERVATIONS), RequestType.CREATE_RESERVATION (INDIVIDUAL/FAMILY and guide-led GROUP), the RequestType.CANCEL_RESERVATION/RequestType.CONFIRM_RESERVATION lifecycle ops, RequestType.UPDATE_RESERVATION (reschedule), and the waiting-list ops (RequestType.JOIN_WAITLIST/RequestType.LEAVE_WAITLIST/ RequestType.ACCEPT_GRAB). Status transitions are gated by isLegalTransition(common.dto.ReservationStatus, common.dto.ReservationStatus) so the rules live in one place and are enforced server-side regardless of the client UI.

Waiting list. A waitlist entry is a reservation in ReservationStatus.WAITING plus a waiting_list_entry row; WAITING reservations do not consume capacity (see ReservationDAO.availableCapacity(int, java.lang.String)). When a PENDING/CONFIRMED reservation is cancelled, offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String) offers the freed slot to the FIFO-first waiting party that fits and gives them a one-hour window to ACCEPT_GRAB (flip to CONFIRMED). Declining (LEAVE_WAITLIST during an active offer) or letting the window lapse (expireOverdueOffers()) advances the offer to the next eligible party. The expiry sweep is timer-free here — a later Scheduler session drives it on an interval.

Realtime push. After each successful mutation the controller re-fetches the persisted row and broadcasts a ServerEvent for entity "reservation" (id = reservation id) through SubscriptionRegistry, reusing the exact mechanism the order domain uses — see publishReservation(common.dto.ServerEvent). Subscribers get the committed row.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    expireOverdueOffers()
    Sweeps every grab offer whose deadline has passed: the offeree forfeited, so each lapsed entry is removed, its now-orphaned WAITING reservation is CANCELLED (via ReservationDAO.updateStatus(int, common.dto.ReservationStatus), which also stamps status_changed_at), and the grab is advanced to the next eligible waiting party for that same park/date (via offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String)).
    int
    expireOverdueOffers(boolean force)
    Force-aware variant of expireOverdueOffers().
    int
    expireUnconfirmedReservations()
    Auto-cancels every PENDING reservation that was reminded to confirm but did not do so within SchedulerConfig.getConfirmTimeoutMinutes() minutes.
    int
    expireUnconfirmedReservations(boolean force)
    Force-aware variant of expireUnconfirmedReservations().

    Whether an entry's grab offer is still live: it has been offered and its expiry is in the future.

    Basic server-side email sanity check for the booking contact: non-blank and containing both an '@' and a '.'.

    Basic server-side phone check for the booking contact: it must carry at least ten digits (a national mobile number's worth), ignoring any formatting such as dashes, spaces, parentheses or a leading '+'.

    • handledTypes

      public Set<RequestType> handledTypes()

      Returns:
      the result produced by the operation

    • handle

      public ServerResponse handle(ClientRequest request, ClientSession session)
      Handles the supplied request and returns the appropriate server response.
      Specified by:
      handle in interface DomainController
      Parameters:
      request - value supplied to the operation
      session - value supplied to the operation
      Returns:
      the result produced by the operation

    • isValidEmail

      private boolean isValidEmail(String email)
      Basic server-side email sanity check for the booking contact: non-blank and containing both an '@' and a '.'. Deliberately lenient (this is a teaching prototype, not an RFC validator) but enough to reject the empty or obviously malformed addresses the client guard also blocks, so a crafted request cannot slip a contactless booking past the server.
      Parameters:
      email - the candidate email from the request, possibly null
      Returns:
      true if the email looks well-formed enough to accept

    • isValidPhone

      private boolean isValidPhone(String phone)
      Basic server-side phone check for the booking contact: it must carry at least ten digits (a national mobile number's worth), ignoring any formatting such as dashes, spaces, parentheses or a leading '+'. Deliberately lenient on punctuation but strict enough to reject a stray "3" the client guard also blocks, mirroring isValidEmail(java.lang.String).
      Parameters:
      phone - the candidate phone from the request, possibly null
      Returns:
      true if it contains at least ten digits

    • validateFamilyVisit

      private ServerResponse validateFamilyVisit(VisitType visitType, long visitorId, int partySize)
      Enforces the clarified family-visit rule: FAMILY visits belong to subscribers and cannot exceed the subscriber's registered family size.
      Parameters:
      visitType - the requested visit type
      visitorId - the visitor who owns the booking
      partySize - requested number of people
      Returns:
      an error response when invalid, otherwise null

    • isLegalTransition

      private boolean isLegalTransition(ReservationStatus current, ReservationStatus target)

    • resolveEntry

      private WaitlistEntryDTO resolveEntry(ClientRequest request)
      Resolves the waiting-list entry a request targets. Accepts either a reservationId (the handle the client already holds for the booking) or, as a fallback, a direct entryId.
      Parameters:
      request - the client request carrying reservationId or entryId
      Returns:
      the matching WaitlistEntryDTO, or null if neither key resolves

    • isOfferActive

      private boolean isOfferActive(WaitlistEntryDTO entry)
      Whether an entry's grab offer is still live: it has been offered and its expiry is in the future. Compared against the JVM clock — the same clock the expiry is computed from in offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String) — so the window is internally consistent.
      Parameters:
      entry - the entry to test (assumed to have a non-null grabOfferedAt)
      Returns:
      true if the offer has not yet lapsed

    • offerGrabToNext

      private void offerGrabToNext(int parkId, String visitDate)
      Offers a freed slot to the next waiting visitor. Recomputes the live capacity for the park/date, finds the FIFO-first WAITING entry whose party fits (and that does not already hold an offer), marks it offered for SchedulerConfig.getGrabWindowMinutes() minute(s), and notifies that visitor. A no-op when no capacity is free or no eligible entry is waiting (e.g. the head party is too large to fit the freed slot — the must-fit rule skips it rather than blocking the queue).

      Called after any event that frees a real slot: a cancelled PENDING/CONFIRMED reservation, a declined offer, or a lapsed offer.

      Parameters:
      parkId - the park a slot freed up for
      visitDate - the visit date a slot freed up for, ISO yyyy-MM-dd

    • expireOverdueOffers

      public int expireOverdueOffers()
      Sweeps every grab offer whose deadline has passed: the offeree forfeited, so each lapsed entry is removed, its now-orphaned WAITING reservation is CANCELLED (via ReservationDAO.updateStatus(int, common.dto.ReservationStatus), which also stamps status_changed_at), and the grab is advanced to the next eligible waiting party for that same park/date (via offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String)).

      Cancelling the forfeited reservation resolves the orphaned-WAITING loose end: a WAITING reservation never consumes capacity, so this does not change the headroom offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String) recomputes for the next party.

      Driven on a fixed interval by the Scheduler (WaitlistGrabExpiryJob); also exercisable manually from the server console.

      Equivalent to expireOverdueOffers(false).

      Returns:
      the number of lapsed offers processed (0 when none were due)

    • expireOverdueOffers

      public int expireOverdueOffers(boolean force)
      Force-aware variant of expireOverdueOffers(). With force every currently-active grab offer is treated as expired (see WaitlistDAO.findExpiredOffers(boolean)) — the manual "run now" path, which expires an outstanding offer and advances the queue immediately rather than waiting for the grab window to lapse.
      Parameters:
      force - true to expire any active offer now; false for the normal lapsed sweep
      Returns:
      the number of offers processed (0 when none were eligible)

    • expireUnconfirmedReservations

      public int expireUnconfirmedReservations()
      Auto-cancels every PENDING reservation that was reminded to confirm but did not do so within SchedulerConfig.getConfirmTimeoutMinutes() minutes. For each lapsed reservation this mirrors the CANCEL_RESERVATION path: flip it to CANCELLED (stamping status_changed_at), broadcast the change so an online visitor's screen updates, notify the visitor, and offer the freed slot to the waiting list via offerGrabToNext(int, java.lang.String).

      Only still-PENDING rows are touched (see ReservationDAO.findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(int)), so a visitor who confirmed in time (PENDING→CONFIRMED) is never cancelled by this sweep — the paired ReminderJob sends the reminder, this sweep enforces its deadline.

      Driven on a fixed interval by the Scheduler (ConfirmTimeoutJob); also exercisable manually from the server console.

      Equivalent to expireUnconfirmedReservations(false).

      Returns:
      the number of reservations auto-cancelled (0 when none were due)

    • expireUnconfirmedReservations

      public int expireUnconfirmedReservations(boolean force)
      Force-aware variant of expireUnconfirmedReservations(). With force every still-PENDING reservation is cancelled now, skipping the reminder/confirm-timeout window (see ReservationDAO.findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(int, boolean)) — the manual "run now" path. Each cancellation still broadcasts the change, notifies the visitor, and offers the freed slot to the waiting list, exactly as the timed sweep does.
      Parameters:
      force - true to cancel every PENDING now; false for the normal window sweep
      Returns:
      the number of reservations auto-cancelled (0 when none were eligible)

    • publishReservation

      private void publishReservation(ServerEvent event)
      Fans a reservation change out to every client subscribed to that reservation id, via the shared SubscriptionRegistry (the same realtime-push path the order domain uses). The subscription key's entity string is always "reservation" so it matches what the client screens subscribe to.
      Parameters:
      event - the change to broadcast; its entityId is the reservation id
